Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast London & South East England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Monday, risk of morning showers, then bright. Today: Any morning showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with bright or sunny spells developing for the afternoon. Cloud increasing later in the evening with a risk of some showery rain. Mostly light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 24 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Winds remaining generally light. Minimum temperature 13 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, sunny spells developing. Thicker cloud with some rain gradually extending east through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 26 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some patchy rain. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, perhaps locally hot, and feeling increasingly humid. Thundery showers possible. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast North West England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Turning more unsettled through the week. Today: A bright start with some good spells of sunshine. However, skies will cloud over through the morning, perhaps with a few showers for a time. Warm bright spells developing again in the afternoon. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: Clouding over again this evening with a few light showers possible overnight. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud with light winds. Minimum temperature 14 °C. Tuesday: Cloudy on Tuesday with some light showers at first. A few brighter spells possible for a time, before a spell of heavy rain moves in from the south later. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Unsettled at times over the coming days with showers and spells of rain. However, some drier, warmer and brighter interludes are possible too. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. There is advice if you are at risk from higher levels of pollution . Pollen Daily level High Pollen forecast for the UK Pollen This shows the pollen level for the region this location is in. More information about regions is on our pollen forecast page . The forecast uses 4 levels: Low Moderate High Very high Wind Daily highest gust 25mph Wind gust (mph) This shows the highest wind speed that you should encounter today. Feels like Daily high 18°C Maximum feels like temperature: 18 degrees Celsius; Daily low 13°C Minimum feels like temperature: 13 degrees Celsius; Feels like temperature Feels like temperature considers factors such as wind speed and humidity. This gives you a better idea of how the temperature will actually feel. Next rain Chance of rain 60% Rain 40% at 10am Chance of precipitation This shows when the next chance of rain or other precipitation is. Humidity Daily high 96% Daily low 74% Humidity Humidity is the amount of water vapor in the air. If there is a lot of water vapour, the humidity will be high. The higher the percentage of humidity, the wetter it will feel outside. UV Daily highest level High Seek shade during midday hours, cover up and wear sunscreen. UV UV exposure index and the protection required to help keep you safe: - No risk of UV It’s safe to stay outside. 1-2 Low You can safely stay outside. Consider sunscreen in direct sunlight. 3-5 Moderate Take care during midday hours and do not spend too much time in the sun unprotected. Sunscreen advised. 6-7 High Seek shade during midday hours, cover up and wear sunscreen. 8-10 Very high Spend time in the shade between 11am and 3pm. Shirt, sunscreen and hat are essential. 11 Extreme Avoid being outside during midday hours. Shirt, sunscreen and hat essential. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast East Midlands weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Bright on Monday, risk of showers later. Today: Any early showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with some hazy sunshine developing through the morning. Cloud increasing into the evening with a risk of some showery rain, perhaps giving the odd heavier burst. Light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 21 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Fog patches across the Peak District. Light winds. Minimum temperature 11 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, bright spells developing. However, thicker cloud and rain arriving from the west through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 24 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some rain likely. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, and perhaps more humid. Winds generally light. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast North West England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Turning more unsettled through the week. Today: A bright start with some good spells of sunshine. However, skies will cloud over through the morning, perhaps with a few showers for a time. Warm bright spells developing again in the afternoon. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: Clouding over again this evening with a few light showers possible overnight. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud with light winds. Minimum temperature 14 °C. Tuesday: Cloudy on Tuesday with some light showers at first. A few brighter spells possible for a time, before a spell of heavy rain moves in from the south later. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Unsettled at times over the coming days with showers and spells of rain. However, some drier, warmer and brighter interludes are possible too. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Beach safety Check if a beach is patrolled by RNLI lifeguards More beach safety tips Water safety from the RNLI What to do if caught in a rip current Float to live if you’re struggling in water UK video forecast Wales weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Rather cloudy today. Today: A bright start for much of Wales with some good sunny spells. Cloud will thicken as the day goes on, with many places holding onto cloudy skies through the rest of the day. Feeling warm. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Tonight: Low cloud will spread across Wales during the evening and overnight with a few spots of drizzle possible at times. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud. Minimum temperature 15 °C. Tuesday: A grey and murky start but largely dry. Outbreaks of rain will gradually move in from the south through the day, turning heavy over the hills. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Further spells of rain are likely over the next few days, but there will also be some drier and brighter interludes at times. Feeling warm and increasingly humid. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Yorkshire & Humber weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Bright early Monday, risk of rain later. Today: Generally dry with some hazy sunshine through the morning. Cloud increasing from the afternoon with a risk of rain, perhaps turning heavier and thundery through the evening. Light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: Outbreaks of rain, initially persistent with some heavier bursts, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a cloudy, damp start to Tuesday, with hill fog across the Pennines. Winds remaining light. Minimum temperature 9 °C. Tuesday: Gradually brightening after a cloudy, damp start, and eventually feeling warmer. However, thicker cloud and rain returning from the west through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 22 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Often rather cloudy and breezy during this period, with outbreaks of rain at times. The best of any sunshine perhaps on Thursday, and east of hills. Gradually turning warmer. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Dumfries, Galloway, Lothian & Borders weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Mostly dry and bright. Rain arriving later. Today: A dry and bright morning with sunny spells turning increasingly hazy. Becoming cloudy in the afternoon with a few outbreaks of rain arriving later in the west. Winds light. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Tonight: A rather cloudy evening and night with outbreaks of rain, mostly light, but perhaps briefly moderate or heavy for a time. Mist or fog patches developing around western coasts later. Minimum temperature 11 °C. Tuesday: Rather cloudy with a few spots of light rain, but some drier, brighter spells developing in the afternoon. Outbreaks of rain will then spread northeast during the evening and overnight. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: A fairly unsettled and changeable few days with a series of showery or occasionally more persistent bands of rain spreading up from the southwest Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Today: A bright start for much of Wales with some good sunny spells. Cloud will thicken as the day goes on, with many places holding onto cloudy skies through the rest of the day. Feeling warm. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Tonight: Low cloud will spread across Wales during the evening and overnight with a few spots of drizzle possible at times. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud. Minimum temperature 15 °C. Tuesday: A grey and murky start but largely dry. Outbreaks of rain will gradually move in from the south through the day, turning heavy over the hills. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Further spells of rain are likely over the next few days, but there will also be some drier and brighter interludes at times. Feeling warm and increasingly humid. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Wales weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Rather cloudy today. Today: A bright start for much of Wales with some good sunny spells. Cloud will thicken as the day goes on, with many places holding onto cloudy skies through the rest of the day. Feeling warm. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Tonight: Low cloud will spread across Wales during the evening and overnight with a few spots of drizzle possible at times. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud. Minimum temperature 15 °C. Tuesday: A grey and murky start but largely dry. Outbreaks of rain will gradually move in from the south through the day, turning heavy over the hills. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Further spells of rain are likely over the next few days, but there will also be some drier and brighter interludes at times. Feeling warm and increasingly humid. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast East Midlands weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Bright on Monday, risk of showers later. Today: Any early showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with some hazy sunshine developing through the morning. Cloud increasing into the evening with a risk of some showery rain, perhaps giving the odd heavier burst. Light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 21 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Fog patches across the Peak District. Light winds. Minimum temperature 11 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, bright spells developing. However, thicker cloud and rain arriving from the west through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 24 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some rain likely. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, and perhaps more humid. Winds generally light. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Northern Ireland weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Bright, mainly dry. A few spots of rain possible. Today: A mainly dry and bright to start. Cloud will thicken through the day to give the odd spot of rain at times. Gentle east to southeast breezes. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: A rather cloudy evening with some patchy light rain possible at times. Remaining mainly cloudy through the rest of the night with a few mist or fog patches developing. Minimum temperature 12 °C. Tuesday: Bright but with few spots of light rain not to be ruled out, though many places staying dry until outbreaks of rain spread across from the southwest by late afternoon. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: A fairly unsettled and changeable few days with a series of showery or occasionally more persistent bands of rain spreading up from the southwest Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 4:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Yorkshire & Humber weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Bright early Monday, risk of rain later. Today: Generally dry with some hazy sunshine through the morning. Cloud increasing from the afternoon with a risk of rain, perhaps turning heavier and thundery through the evening. Light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: Outbreaks of rain, initially persistent with some heavier bursts, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a cloudy, damp start to Tuesday, with hill fog across the Pennines. Winds remaining light. Minimum temperature 9 °C. Tuesday: Gradually brightening after a cloudy, damp start, and eventually feeling warmer. However, thicker cloud and rain returning from the west through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 22 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Often rather cloudy and breezy during this period, with outbreaks of rain at times. The best of any sunshine perhaps on Thursday, and east of hills. Gradually turning warmer. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast London & South East England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Monday, risk of morning showers, then bright. Today: Any morning showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with bright or sunny spells developing for the afternoon. Cloud increasing later in the evening with a risk of some showery rain. Mostly light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 24 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Winds remaining generally light. Minimum temperature 13 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, sunny spells developing. Thicker cloud with some rain gradually extending east through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 26 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some patchy rain. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, perhaps locally hot, and feeling increasingly humid. Thundery showers possible. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast East of England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Monday, risk of morning showers, then bright. Today: Any morning showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with bright or sunny spells developing for the afternoon. Cloud increasing through the evening with a risk of some showery rain later. Mostly light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 22 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Winds remaining generally light. Minimum temperature 10 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, sunny spells developing. Thicker cloud and some rain arriving from the west through the late evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 25 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some rain likely. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, perhaps locally hot, and feeling increasingly humid. Thundery showers possible. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Today: A bright start with some good spells of sunshine. However, skies will cloud over through the morning, perhaps with a few showers for a time. Warm bright spells developing again in the afternoon. Maximum temperature 25 °C. Tonight: Clouding over again this evening with a few light showers possible overnight. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 7am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast London & South East England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Monday, risk of morning showers, then bright. Today: Any morning showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with bright or sunny spells developing for the afternoon. Cloud increasing later in the evening with a risk of some showery rain. Mostly light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 24 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Winds remaining generally light. Minimum temperature 13 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, sunny spells developing. Thicker cloud with some rain gradually extending east through the evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 26 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some patchy rain. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, perhaps locally hot, and feeling increasingly humid. Thundery showers possible. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times. Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast Wales weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Rather cloudy today. Today: A bright start for much of Wales with some good sunny spells. Cloud will thicken as the day goes on, with many places holding onto cloudy skies through the rest of the day. Feeling warm. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Tonight: Low cloud will spread across Wales during the evening and overnight with a few spots of drizzle possible at times. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud. Minimum temperature 15 °C. Tuesday: A grey and murky start but largely dry. Outbreaks of rain will gradually move in from the south through the day, turning heavy over the hills. Maximum temperature 20 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Further spells of rain are likely over the next few days, but there will also be some drier and brighter interludes at times. Feeling warm and increasingly humid. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times.
